title: Acronia alopex
taxonomy: Acronia alopex is a species within the Rubiaceae family, a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder or coffee family. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest families of flowering plants, with over 13,000 known species. As a member of this family, Acronia alopex shares characteristics with other species, such as five-lobed leaves and small, fragrant flowers. The species is classified within the genus Acronia, which is a group of flowering plants native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Acronia alopex into these groups highlights its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Rubiaceae family.
